gaps.message: data-owning capability missing retention runtime proof

evidence.contract: GET /admin; GET /api/admin/backup; GET /api/admin/clickthrough; GET /api/admin/governance/catalog; POST /api/admin/governance/catalog; POST /api/admin/governance/catalog/classify; GET /api/admin/governance/dsr; POST /api/admin/governance/dsr; POST /api/admin/governance/dsr/:dsrId/fulfill; GET /api/admin/provider-bindings; GET /api/admin/security; POST /api/admin/sub-tenants; GET /api/admin/tenants; POST /api/admin/tenants/auth-settings-credential; GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/auth-settings-credential/readiness; POST /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/auth-settings-credential/repair; POST /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/auth-settings-credential/rotate; GET /api/admin/tenants/resources; GET /api/organisation/profile; PATCH /api/organisation/profile; GET /organisation/profile
evidence.readinessCheck: tenant-context readiness kind (always ready once resolved)
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved
evidence.acceptablePartialRouteReason: Tenant record + FQDN lifecycle is fully delivered and proven (proof:domain-identity-matrix; proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution); provisioning operates via the system-admin surface. A richer self-service provisioning UI is not part of this capability — the '(partial UI)' note is cosmetic, not a closure gap.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Tenant host identity lifecycle is explicit: apex/reserved/invalid/malformed hosts resolve no tenant, seeded slug FQDNs resolve to hostSource=slug, and active verified custom domains resolve to hostSource=custom_domain. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/domain-identity-matrix-runtime-proof.ts and tenant-custom-domain-resolution runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes organisation id, slug/FQDN, realmName tenant-{organisationId}, hostSource slug|custom_domain|fixture, and tenant_domains registry states for custom FQDNs. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/tenant-resolver.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant provisioning is operator-controlled by platform.tenants.read/create; host identity read is deterministic request context and does not grant data access without route permission checks. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/server/pipeline.ts tenant resolution flow.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is POST /api/admin/tenants for provisioning and GET /api/host-identity for request host identity; runtime resolution consumes Host/X-Forwarded-Host. Evidence: capability record, routes, and domain-identity-matrix proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Host validation classifies apex, slug, local slug, reserved, unknown, malformed, host-with-port, and possible custom-domain hosts; X-Forwarded-Host precedence is proven. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts and domain-identity-matrix runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid, unknown, reserved, apex, inactive custom, pending custom, and disabled custom hosts resolve to null tenant context rather than a partial or fallback tenant. Evidence: tenant-resolver tests and tenant-custom-domain-resolution runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Provisioning is audited at the tenant provisioning surface; host identity resolution itself is read-only and has no mutation audit. Evidence: capability auditAction and tenant resolver/pipeline read-only path.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready once the resolver and registry are available; proofs skip honestly only when required seeded/local substrate is absent and do not fake tenant resolution. Evidence: domain-identity-matrix and tenant-custom-domain-resolution runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:domain-identity-matrix validates classification plus live slug resolution; proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution validates live tenant_domains resolution, inactive/pending/disabled rejection, and unique enabled-domain guard. Proof level: 4 live substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI generation must treat tenant identity as a context primitive: display current tenant/host source, distinguish apex/global vs tenant contexts, expose provisioning only to platform tenant operators, and never infer a tenant from invalid or untrusted hosts.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0029, ADR-0021; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: User identity + tenant membership
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.contract: /api/org/members*; GET /admin/members; GET /api/org/developer; GET /api/org/email-sender; PATCH /api/org/email-sender; GET /api/org/email-sender/readiness; POST /api/org/email-sender/test; GET /api/org/features; PATCH /api/org/features/:featureKey; GET /api/org/members; DELETE /api/org/members/:userId; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId; GET /api/org/members/:userId/external-identities; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId/status; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId/username; POST /api/org/members/invite; POST /api/org/members/resend-invite; GET /api/org/readiness
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Membership lifecycle is explicit: invite validates and creates pending invitation for a new user or direct membership for an existing user, invited resolves to active on login, role/status/username can be updated, disabled can be re-enabled, and remove deletes the membership. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/members.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is tenant-scoped Membership with userId, organisationId, tenant role, optional username, status invited|active|disabled, createdAt, updatedAt, lastLoginAt, invitedBy, plus pending invitation rows. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/src/index.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.members.read, tenant.members.invite, tenant.members.update_role, or tenant.members.delete; manager has legacy member.* bundle only and does not receive tenant.members.* route permissions. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/members, POST /api/org/members/invite, PATCH/DELETE /api/org/members/:userId, PATCH username/status, POST resend-invite, and GET external-identities. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Email format is validated and lowercased; system-admin is rejected as a tenant membership role; username format and status transitions are explicit; last tenant-admin cannot be demoted, disabled, or removed. Evidence: members unit tests and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_body, conflict, already_invited, not_found, last_admin_cannot_be_demoted, last_admin_cannot_be_removed, last_admin_cannot_be_disabled, invalid_transition, and ok; routes map them to 400/409/404/422/204/201 responses. Evidence: members usecase and routes.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Member invite, role change, username/status changes, resend invite, and remove emit audit only after validation and precondition reads; audit failure aborts mutation and conflict paths emit no misleading audit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Membership readiness is invariant-ready once tenant context and Postgres identity tables are available; list reads use tenant context and pending invitation queries always filter by organisation_id. Evidence: members usecase comments and tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts, members-v2.test.ts, packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts; substrate proof: postgres-identity-repository and organisation profile/member route coverage in test:platform-api. Proof level: 3 state-machine/permission validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose member and pending-invitation tables, invite form, role/status/username edit actions, resend and remove actions, external identity read-only details, last-admin guard states, duplicate/conflict states, validation errors, permission-denied states, and audit-before-change expectations.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0036, ADR-0038; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: User identity + tenant membership
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing retention runtime proof

evidence.contract: /api/org/members*; GET /admin/members; GET /api/org/developer; GET /api/org/email-sender; PATCH /api/org/email-sender; GET /api/org/email-sender/readiness; POST /api/org/email-sender/test; GET /api/org/features; PATCH /api/org/features/:featureKey; GET /api/org/members; DELETE /api/org/members/:userId; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId; GET /api/org/members/:userId/external-identities; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId/status; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId/username; POST /api/org/members/invite; POST /api/org/members/resend-invite; GET /api/org/readiness
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Membership lifecycle is explicit: invite validates and creates pending invitation for a new user or direct membership for an existing user, invited resolves to active on login, role/status/username can be updated, disabled can be re-enabled, and remove deletes the membership. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/members.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is tenant-scoped Membership with userId, organisationId, tenant role, optional username, status invited|active|disabled, createdAt, updatedAt, lastLoginAt, invitedBy, plus pending invitation rows. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/src/index.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.members.read, tenant.members.invite, tenant.members.update_role, or tenant.members.delete; manager has legacy member.* bundle only and does not receive tenant.members.* route permissions. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/members, POST /api/org/members/invite, PATCH/DELETE /api/org/members/:userId, PATCH username/status, POST resend-invite, and GET external-identities. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Email format is validated and lowercased; system-admin is rejected as a tenant membership role; username format and status transitions are explicit; last tenant-admin cannot be demoted, disabled, or removed. Evidence: members unit tests and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_body, conflict, already_invited, not_found, last_admin_cannot_be_demoted, last_admin_cannot_be_removed, last_admin_cannot_be_disabled, invalid_transition, and ok; routes map them to 400/409/404/422/204/201 responses. Evidence: members usecase and routes.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Member invite, role change, username/status changes, resend invite, and remove emit audit only after validation and precondition reads; audit failure aborts mutation and conflict paths emit no misleading audit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Membership readiness is invariant-ready once tenant context and Postgres identity tables are available; list reads use tenant context and pending invitation queries always filter by organisation_id. Evidence: members usecase comments and tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts, members-v2.test.ts, packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts; substrate proof: postgres-identity-repository and organisation profile/member route coverage in test:platform-api. Proof level: 3 state-machine/permission validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose member and pending-invitation tables, invite form, role/status/username edit actions, resend and remove actions, external identity read-only details, last-admin guard states, duplicate/conflict states, validation errors, permission-denied states, and audit-before-change expectations.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0036, ADR-0038; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

evidence.contract: /api/org/members*; GET /admin/members; GET /api/org/developer; GET /api/org/email-sender; PATCH /api/org/email-sender; GET /api/org/email-sender/readiness; POST /api/org/email-sender/test; GET /api/org/features; PATCH /api/org/features/:featureKey; GET /api/org/members; DELETE /api/org/members/:userId; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId; GET /api/org/members/:userId/external-identities; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId/status; PATCH /api/org/members/:userId/username; POST /api/org/members/invite; POST /api/org/members/resend-invite; GET /api/org/readiness
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Membership lifecycle is explicit: invite validates and creates pending invitation for a new user or direct membership for an existing user, invited resolves to active on login, role/status/username can be updated, disabled can be re-enabled, and remove deletes the membership. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/members.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is tenant-scoped Membership with userId, organisationId, tenant role, optional username, status invited|active|disabled, createdAt, updatedAt, lastLoginAt, invitedBy, plus pending invitation rows. Evidence: packages/domain-identity/src/index.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.members.read, tenant.members.invite, tenant.members.update_role, or tenant.members.delete; manager has legacy member.* bundle only and does not receive tenant.members.* route permissions. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/members, POST /api/org/members/invite, PATCH/DELETE /api/org/members/:userId, PATCH username/status, POST resend-invite, and GET external-identities. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Email format is validated and lowercased; system-admin is rejected as a tenant membership role; username format and status transitions are explicit; last tenant-admin cannot be demoted, disabled, or removed. Evidence: members unit tests and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_body, conflict, already_invited, not_found, last_admin_cannot_be_demoted, last_admin_cannot_be_removed, last_admin_cannot_be_disabled, invalid_transition, and ok; routes map them to 400/409/404/422/204/201 responses. Evidence: members usecase and routes.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Member invite, role change, username/status changes, resend invite, and remove emit audit only after validation and precondition reads; audit failure aborts mutation and conflict paths emit no misleading audit. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Membership readiness is invariant-ready once tenant context and Postgres identity tables are available; list reads use tenant context and pending invitation queries always filter by organisation_id. Evidence: members usecase comments and tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/members.test.ts, members-v2.test.ts, packages/domain-identity/tests/domain-identity.test.ts; substrate proof: postgres-identity-repository and organisation profile/member route coverage in test:platform-api. Proof level: 3 state-machine/permission validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose member and pending-invitation tables, invite form, role/status/username edit actions, resend and remove actions, external identity read-only details, last-admin guard states, duplicate/conflict states, validation errors, permission-denied states, and audit-before-change expectations.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0036, ADR-0038; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: End-user profile + preferences self-service
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Profile lifecycle is default -> updated own profile; preferences lifecycle is default empty -> updated enabled/disabled channel/category preferences -> read back; notification dispatch uses enabled preferences as sent and disabled preferences as suppressed. Evidence: profile, notifications unit tests, profile-self-service and notification-preferences runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Profile state is tenant+user scoped displayName, locale, timezone; preference state is tenant+user scoped channel, category, enabled; dispatch state is durable log status sent|suppressed|failed. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/profile-repository.ts and notification-repository.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Self-service reads use profile.read_self and writes use profile.update_self; userId is the authenticated session subject and never a path/body parameter. Operator notification test uses platform.notifications.read/write outside this self-service surface. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and notification-dispatch runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contracts are GET/PATCH /api/me/profile and GET/PATCH /api/me/notification-preferences; notification readiness/test contracts support the preference proof. Evidence: routes.ts and docs/api/openapi.json.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Profile update rejects empty displayName; route schema rejects invalid profile/preferences payloads; dispatch rejects secret-bearing payload fields; PATCH without tenant context returns NO_TENANT. Evidence: profile unit tests and notification-dispatch runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Validation failures return safe VALIDATION_ERROR/ValidationError responses; no-tenant profile/preference writes return 400 NO_TENANT; secret-bearing notification payloads raise api.error.secretFieldNotNotifiable. Evidence: profile.ts, notifications.ts, and route proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Profile updates emit profile.updated on user_profile before change; preference updates emit notification preferences audit; operator test sends emit notification.tested. Evidence: profile and notifications unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Profile and preferences are always-on once the Postgres substrate is migrated; live proofs skip honestly if Postgres is unavailable and verify no secret-bearing columns plus RLS tenant isolation. Evidence: profile-self-service and notification-preferences runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/profile.test.ts and notifications.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:profile-self-service, proof:notification-preferences, proof:notification-dispatch. Proof level: 4 live Postgres/RLS and live route-handler validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose Account profile fields displayName/locale/timezone, notification preference channel/category toggles, read defaults, update validation, own-user-only semantics, no-tenant/operator default states, permission-denied states, and no secret-bearing payload display.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0068; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant groups
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.route: admin UI semantics proven via the Semantic Reference Harness (proof:ui-semantic-groups)
evidence.v2Target: reuse-and-extend: V1 API+permissions+contract are the semantic source; V2 must add the missing admin UI slice (no behaviour change)
evidence.acceptablePartialRouteReason: UI semantic gap closed by the Semantic Reference Harness headless journey (proof:ui-semantic-groups) — machine-readable definition + passing journey — establishing the V2 build contract (states/permissions/validation/a11y). A shipped V1 admin page is intentionally out of scope for this semantic closure.
evidence.closedBy: V1-completion acceleration slice — Semantic Reference Harness

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Group lifecycle is explicit: list existing Keycloak groups, create after name validation and duplicate check, update after existence and conflict checks, and delete after protected-name guard. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/groups.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/groups.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Group state is Keycloak group id, name, and path in the tenant realm; reserved names are protected and groups are not persisted in Postgres. Evidence: GroupsAdapterPort and groups unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes require tenant.groups.read/create/update/delete, are tenant-scoped, and declare organisation:groups UMA resource and matching scopes. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts and groups unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract surface is GET/POST /api/org/groups and PATCH/DELETE /api/org/groups/:groupId with tenant credential-backed KeycloakRealmAdminAdapter. Evidence: routes.ts and groups usecase.

gaps.subject: Audit of privileged access
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Privileged audit lifecycle is read-only contextual query over durable audit rows: choose logical resource, map to stored resource and per-context permission, query tenant-scoped audit events, redact secret metadata, and return bounded results. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/audit.ts and audit unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is AuditEventDto with id, action, actorId, resource, resourceId, timestamp, and redacted metadata; logical resources map to member/config/feature/auth_settings contexts. Evidence: audit usecase.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Route requires coarse tenant.audit.read and the usecase enforces per-context permissions such as tenant.members.read, tenant.config.read, tenant.features.read, and tenant.auth.settings.read. Evidence: routes.ts and audit unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract is GET /api/org/audit with resource, resourceId, action, actorId, from, to, and limit query parameters; tenant id comes from the resolved session tenant, never from the frontend. Evidence: routes.ts and audit usecase.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown logical resources are invalid; missing per-context permission is forbidden; limit is clamped to 1..200; resourceId/action/actor/date filters are optional. Evidence: audit usecase and tests.

semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready for contextual audit reads; the broader live history proof validates read-only projection, source isolation, secret exclusion, pagination, and no mutation against live Postgres. Evidence: proof:history.

semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose audit panels with logical resource filters, resource/action/actor/time filters, bounded pagination, forbidden/invalid states, tenant-only results, redacted metadata, and no mutation controls on read-only audit events.

evidence.auditAction: config change audited; tenant.config.changed webhook event
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Config lifecycle is definition active/deprecated/internal, list effective values from default or tenant_override, set tenant override with audit-first upsert, clear override with audit-first delete, and emit tenant.config.changed webhook event best-effort after mutation. Evidence: platform-config usecase/tests and routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is PLATFORM_CONFIG_DEFINITIONS plus tenant_settings storageKey values, source default|tenant_override, valueType string|enum|boolean|json, allowedValues, tenantOverridable, per-definition permissions, lifecycle, and feature flag compatibility transforms. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/config/registry.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: GET /api/org/config requires tenant.config.read and additionally filters definitions by requiredPermissionRead; PATCH/DELETE require tenant.config.write and usecase-enforce each definition's requiredPermissionWrite. Feature definitions require tenant.features.*. Evidence: platform-config tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/config?category=, PATCH /api/org/config/:key with {value}, and DELETE /api/org/config/:key; response items include serialised definition, value, and source. Evidence: routes.ts and contracts-admin config DTOs.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown keys, non-overridable definitions, missing value, enum values outside allowedValues, type mismatches, category filters, and insufficient per-definition permissions are explicit. Evidence: platform-config tests and contracts-admin validateConfigValue.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Routes map invalid_body to 400, not_found to 404, not_overridable to 422, forbidden to 403, no tenant to 400, and successful writes/deletes to 204; webhook fan-out failure is best-effort and does not roll back config mutation. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set emits the definition auditAction, clear emits ConfigValueCleared, both before tenant_settings mutation with key/value metadata; audit failure prevents DB writes. Evidence: platform-config tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready once tenant_settings/Postgres is available; definition registry is pure and serialisable, and history/audit projection is covered by the live history proof. Evidence: registry.ts and proof:history.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/platform-config.test.ts and contracts-admin config validation tests. Adjacent runtime proof: proof:history validates live audit/history projection for config changes. Proof level: 3 for state/validation plus 4 for live history projection.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must generate config controls from definition DTOs, render source default vs tenant_override, category filters, allowed enum values, boolean/json/string editors, per-definition permission/read-only states, validation errors, clear override action, and history/audit links.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0039; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is PLATFORM_CONFIG_DEFINITIONS plus tenant_settings storageKey values, source default|tenant_override, valueType string|enum|boolean|json, allowedValues, tenantOverridable, per-definition permissions, lifecycle, and feature flag compatibility transforms. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/config/registry.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: GET /api/org/config requires tenant.config.read and additionally filters definitions by requiredPermissionRead; PATCH/DELETE require tenant.config.write and usecase-enforce each definition's requiredPermissionWrite. Feature definitions require tenant.features.*. Evidence: platform-config tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/config?category=, PATCH /api/org/config/:key with {value}, and DELETE /api/org/config/:key; response items include serialised definition, value, and source. Evidence: routes.ts and contracts-admin config DTOs.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown keys, non-overridable definitions, missing value, enum values outside allowedValues, type mismatches, category filters, and insufficient per-definition permissions are explicit. Evidence: platform-config tests and contracts-admin validateConfigValue.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Routes map invalid_body to 400, not_found to 404, not_overridable to 422, forbidden to 403, no tenant to 400, and successful writes/deletes to 204; webhook fan-out failure is best-effort and does not roll back config mutation. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set emits the definition auditAction, clear emits ConfigValueCleared, both before tenant_settings mutation with key/value metadata; audit failure prevents DB writes. Evidence: platform-config tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready once tenant_settings/Postgres is available; definition registry is pure and serialisable, and history/audit projection is covered by the live history proof. Evidence: registry.ts and proof:history.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/platform-config.test.ts and contracts-admin config validation tests. Adjacent runtime proof: proof:history validates live audit/history projection for config changes. Proof level: 3 for state/validation plus 4 for live history projection.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must generate config controls from definition DTOs, render source default vs tenant_override, category filters, allowed enum values, boolean/json/string editors, per-definition permission/read-only states, validation errors, clear override action, and history/audit links.

gaps.subject: Relational storage + migrations + RLS
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.permission: server-only; no direct DB from React
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Relational lifecycle is migration-ordered schema creation, tenant-scoped repository access through withTenant, operator access through explicit rls_bypass/system-admin paths, migration lineage tracking, and backup/restore guard coverage. Evidence: migrations tests, repository substrate tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is Postgres public and tenant-scoped tables with organisation_id tenant boundary, RLS policies, migration chain entries, and repository DTOs for each bounded context; no React client can connect directly to the database. Evidence: migrations, adapters-postgres, and substrate repository tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Database access is server-only. Tenant reads/writes are mediated by BFF route permissions and repository context; operator paths use explicit platform permissions and rls_bypass where proven. pgAdmin is global-only clickthrough, not a tenant data API. Evidence: routes and service-clickthrough policy tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: The contract is all data through BFF/GraphQL/repository ports; database tables are not a frontend contract. Migration lineage is captured in data-and-migration-plan and migration tests. Evidence: docs/v2-foundation/data-and-migration-plan.json and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/migrations.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation is performed at API/usecase boundaries before repository mutation; repository tests prove tenant filters/RLS behavior and migrations tests prove on-disk migration presence/lineage. Evidence: platform-api substrate/unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Relational failures surface as route/usecase typed errors where handled, readiness not-ready/degraded where probed, or test/proof failures for migration/RLS regressions; no UI receives raw database errors as semantic state. Evidence: pipeline error handling and readiness tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Application mutations emit domain audit events before repository writes where required; schema changes are tracked by migrations and backup/restore operational evidence rather than per-row UI audit. Evidence: audit tests, migrations tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is Postgres probe plus migration/RLS proof: live proofs honestly skip when Postgres is unavailable and fail on RLS, schema, or migration-chain regressions. Evidence: health handlers, metering/search/backup live proofs, and R17 validator coverage.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Substrate proofs include Postgres identity/organisation repositories, migrations tests, RLS-backed metering/search proofs, and backup-local live proof. Proof level: 4 where live Postgres/RLS substrate is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must never model direct database access; it must derive data states from BFF contracts, show database/readiness/migration failures only through explicit readiness and admin surfaces, and treat pgAdmin as global operator clickthrough with no tenant-scoped CRUD semantics.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0014, ADR-0029; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Application mutations emit domain audit events before repository writes where required; schema changes are tracked by migrations and backup/restore operational evidence rather than per-row UI audit. Evidence: audit tests, migrations tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is Postgres probe plus migration/RLS proof: live proofs honestly skip when Postgres is unavailable and fail on RLS, schema, or migration-chain regressions. Evidence: health handlers, metering/search/backup live proofs, and R17 validator coverage.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Substrate proofs include Postgres identity/organisation repositories, migrations tests, RLS-backed metering/search proofs, and backup-local live proof. Proof level: 4 where live Postgres/RLS substrate is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must never model direct database access; it must derive data states from BFF contracts, show database/readiness/migration failures only through explicit readiness and admin surfaces, and treat pgAdmin as global operator clickthrough with no tenant-scoped CRUD semantics.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0014, ADR-0029; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: PITR, retention, legal hold, data residency
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.capability: PITR, retention, legal hold, data residency
evidence.contract: /api/admin/data/pitr; /api/admin/data/retention-policies; /api/admin/data/legal-holds; /api/admin/data/residency; DELETE /api/admin/data/legal-holds; GET /api/admin/data/legal-holds; POST /api/admin/data/legal-holds; POST /api/admin/data/residency; DELETE /api/admin/data/retention-policies; GET /api/admin/data/retention-policies; POST /api/admin/data/retention-policies
evidence.auditAction: restore actions + retention/legal-hold actions + residency changes/rejections
evidence.readinessCheck: archive health + policy tick + hold guard + residency fail-closed
evidence.v2Target: reuse-and-extend: the data lifecycle surface is now proven and V2 lifts it rather than re-deriving it
evidence.acceptablePartialRouteReason: PITR, retention, legal hold, and residency are implemented and proven locally; no missing placeholder remains.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Retention lifecycle is set enabled policy -> select aged rows -> skip held rows -> record deleted outcomes -> disable policy. Legal hold lifecycle is set active idempotently -> guard consumers preserve rows -> release to released idempotently. Residency lifecycle is set tenant tag -> fail closed on non-home placement. Evidence: retention, legal-hold, and data-residency tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes retention policies with resourceTable, ttlSeconds, filter all|by_status, enabled, retention row outcomes pending|deleted|held; legal holds active|released with reason/setBy/releasedBy; residencyTag per organisation. Evidence: retention/legal-hold/data-residency ports and tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Admin data lifecycle routes require platform.data.*; retention/legal hold/residency are operator-only and tenant-scoped by target organisation id. Evidence: /api/admin/data/* routes.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are /api/admin/data/pitr, /api/admin/data/retention-policies, /api/admin/data/retention-policies/tick, /api/admin/data/legal-holds, and /api/admin/data/residency. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Retention validates holdable resource tables, TTL bounds, and whitelisted filters; legal hold validates holdable table and reason length; residency placement rejects mismatched target region fail-closed. Evidence: unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid lifecycle requests return invalid/validation responses without audit or mutation; missing legal hold release is not_found; scanner/storage/retention consumers see active hold as a forbidden preservation guard; residency mismatch throws a residency error. Evidence: tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set/disable retention, set/release legal hold, and residency changes emit audit-before-change; audit failure prevents writes. Retention evaluation outcomes preserve operational evidence. Evidence: retention and legal-hold tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is archive health plus policy tick plus hold guard plus residency fail-closed; local tests and proof scripts validate state transitions without claiming external PITR beyond guarded local backup/restore evidence. Evidence: proof:pitr-restore-drill, proof:retention, proof:legal-hold, proof:data-residency.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: retention.test.ts, legal-hold.test.ts, data-residency.test.ts. Runtime/route proofs cover backup/PITR control and data-residency aliases. Proof level: 3 state-machine validation, with backup/PITR substrate evidence at level 4 where live Postgres backup is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose retention policy list/set/disable/tick status, legal hold list/set/release with active/released/idempotent states, residency tag and placement errors, operator-only permission gates, audit-backed mutation states, and preservation warnings on held resources.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0064, ADR-0063, ADR-ACT-0297, ADR-ACT-0298; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

Evidence: retention/legal-hold/data-residency ports and tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Admin data lifecycle routes require platform.data.*; retention/legal hold/residency are operator-only and tenant-scoped by target organisation id. Evidence: /api/admin/data/* routes.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are /api/admin/data/pitr, /api/admin/data/retention-policies, /api/admin/data/retention-policies/tick, /api/admin/data/legal-holds, and /api/admin/data/residency. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Retention validates holdable resource tables, TTL bounds, and whitelisted filters; legal hold validates holdable table and reason length; residency placement rejects mismatched target region fail-closed. Evidence: unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid lifecycle requests return invalid/validation responses without audit or mutation; missing legal hold release is not_found; scanner/storage/retention consumers see active hold as a forbidden preservation guard; residency mismatch throws a residency error. Evidence: tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set/disable retention, set/release legal hold, and residency changes emit audit-before-change; audit failure prevents writes. Retention evaluation outcomes preserve operational evidence. Evidence: retention and legal-hold tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is archive health plus policy tick plus hold guard plus residency fail-closed; local tests and proof scripts validate state transitions without claiming external PITR beyond guarded local backup/restore evidence. Evidence: proof:pitr-restore-drill, proof:retention, proof:legal-hold, proof:data-residency.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: retention.test.ts, legal-hold.test.ts, data-residency.test.ts. Runtime/route proofs cover backup/PITR control and data-residency aliases. Proof level: 3 state-machine validation, with backup/PITR substrate evidence at level 4 where live Postgres backup is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose retention policy list/set/disable/tick status, legal hold list/set/release with active/released/idempotent states, residency tag and placement errors, operator-only permission gates, audit-backed mutation states, and preservation warnings on held resources.

gaps.subject: Object storage + tenant prefixes + signed URLs
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.capability: Object storage + tenant prefixes + signed URLs
evidence.contract: /api/org/storage/objects, /api/org/storage/readiness, /api/org/storage/probe; GET /admin/storage; GET /api/org/storage/objects; POST /api/org/storage/objects; DELETE /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey; GET /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey; POST /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey/scan; POST /api/org/storage/probe; GET /api/org/storage/readiness
evidence.v2Target: reuse-and-extend: storage object CRUD, signed URL guard, quota enforcement, legal-hold deletion guard, and ClamAV-backed scan lifecycle are proven

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Object lifecycle is quota-check upload -> store under tenant prefix -> create metadata quarantined -> scan clean|rejected|unavailable -> signed download only when clean -> legal-hold checked delete -> metadata/object removal. Evidence: storage-objects tests and tenant-storage proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is StorageObjectRecord with objectId, organisationId, objectKey, contentType, sizeBytes, scanState uploaded|quarantined|clean|rejected, timestamps, tenant prefix, and quota meter storage.bytes. Evidence: storage object ports/tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Object routes require tenant.storage.read/write; storage readiness/probe are tenant-scoped; quota enforcement requires storage entitlement and legal hold guard enforces preservation before deletion. Evidence: routes.ts and storage-objects tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are /api/org/storage/objects, /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey, /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey/scan, /api/org/storage/readiness, and /api/org/storage/probe. Signed URL contract returns a time-bounded URL only for clean objects. Evidence: routes and tests.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Upload enforces storage.bytes quota with requested byte delta before write; cross-prefix object keys are rejected by S3 adapter guard; download rejects non-clean objects; scanner unavailability fails closed and leaves object quarantined. Evidence: storage-objects tests and tenant-storage-runtime-proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Errors include quotaExceeded, objectNotClean, objectScanUnavailable, legal hold ForbiddenError, missing object null/not-found states, isolation_failed readiness, and provider_unreachable readiness. Evidence: tests and tenant-storage proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Upload/scan/delete emit storage audit events where implemented; clean scans emit StorageObjectScanClean, rejected scans emit StorageObjectRejected, and legal hold prevents lifecycle deletion before destructive mutation. Evidence: storage-objects tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness distinguishes configured, isolation_failed, and provider_unreachable using write/read/delete plus foreign-key rejection probes; local proof honestly notes IAM/bucket-policy isolation is not automated locally. Evidence: tenant-storage-runtime-proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/storage-objects.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:tenant-storage validates live MinIO/in-memory probe and prefix isolation; proof:tenant-storage-objects validates object lifecycle semantics. Proof level: 4 live storage substrate plus 3 object state-machine validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose object list/upload/delete/download, scan state badges, clean-only download enablement, rejected/quarantined/unavailable states, quota exceeded errors, legal-hold deletion guard, readiness/probe states, and tenant-prefix isolation without exposing raw provider credentials.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0049, ADR-0064; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

evidence.capability: Object storage + tenant prefixes + signed URLs
evidence.contract: /api/org/storage/objects, /api/org/storage/readiness, /api/org/storage/probe; GET /admin/storage; GET /api/org/storage/objects; POST /api/org/storage/objects; DELETE /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey; GET /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey; POST /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey/scan; POST /api/org/storage/probe; GET /api/org/storage/readiness
evidence.v2Target: reuse-and-extend: storage object CRUD, signed URL guard, quota enforcement, legal-hold deletion guard, and ClamAV-backed scan lifecycle are proven

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Object lifecycle is quota-check upload -> store under tenant prefix -> create metadata quarantined -> scan clean|rejected|unavailable -> signed download only when clean -> legal-hold checked delete -> metadata/object removal. Evidence: storage-objects tests and tenant-storage proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is StorageObjectRecord with objectId, organisationId, objectKey, contentType, sizeBytes, scanState uploaded|quarantined|clean|rejected, timestamps, tenant prefix, and quota meter storage.bytes. Evidence: storage object ports/tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Object routes require tenant.storage.read/write; storage readiness/probe are tenant-scoped; quota enforcement requires storage entitlement and legal hold guard enforces preservation before deletion. Evidence: routes.ts and storage-objects tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are /api/org/storage/objects, /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey, /api/org/storage/objects/:objectKey/scan, /api/org/storage/readiness, and /api/org/storage/probe. Signed URL contract returns a time-bounded URL only for clean objects. Evidence: routes and tests.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Upload enforces storage.bytes quota with requested byte delta before write; cross-prefix object keys are rejected by S3 adapter guard; download rejects non-clean objects; scanner unavailability fails closed and leaves object quarantined. Evidence: storage-objects tests and tenant-storage-runtime-proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Errors include quotaExceeded, objectNotClean, objectScanUnavailable, legal hold ForbiddenError, missing object null/not-found states, isolation_failed readiness, and provider_unreachable readiness. Evidence: tests and tenant-storage proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Upload/scan/delete emit storage audit events where implemented; clean scans emit StorageObjectScanClean, rejected scans emit StorageObjectRejected, and legal hold prevents lifecycle deletion before destructive mutation. Evidence: storage-objects tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness distinguishes configured, isolation_failed, and provider_unreachable using write/read/delete plus foreign-key rejection probes; local proof honestly notes IAM/bucket-policy isolation is not automated locally. Evidence: tenant-storage-runtime-proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/storage-objects.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:tenant-storage validates live MinIO/in-memory probe and prefix isolation; proof:tenant-storage-objects validates object lifecycle semantics. Proof level: 4 live storage substrate plus 3 object state-machine validation.

gaps.subject: Logs (aggregation + tenant-scoped search)
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing retention runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Log lifecycle is emit structured application log, ingest into Loki, search through the admin log query usecase, constrain result count/direction, and surface tenant observability readiness from bounded ingestion plus tenant-scoped query probes. Evidence: logs-usecase tests and tenant-observability runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is Loki log entries with low-cardinality service/level labels and high-cardinality ids carried as JSON fields, plus readiness fields status, logIngestion, tenantScopedQuery, traceCorrelation, dashboards, otelCollector, metrics, and errorCapture. gaps.subject: Tenant isolation proof
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing retention runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Tenant isolation lifecycle is resolve host identity, bind request context to one organisation, enforce tenant-scoped repositories/RLS/prefixes, reject cross-tenant reads/writes, and verify isolation through domain, storage, search, metering, observability, webhook, and secret proofs. Evidence: isolation proof inventory.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes host classifications apex|slug|reserved|unknown|malformed|custom domain, tenant context organisationId/slug/hostSource, Postgres RLS tenant setting, object storage tenant prefix, and null tenant context for untrusted hosts. Evidence: domain identity and storage proofs.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Permissions are enforced after tenant resolution; unresolved/apex/reserved hosts do not grant tenant context, tenant actors only access their organisation rows/prefixes, and operator bypass requires explicit rls_bypass/system role. Evidence: domain-identity, tenant-storage, and RLS proofs.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are host-identity resolution, tenant-aware route context, storage prefixes, RLS-backed repository ports, and readiness proofs for tenant-scoped substrates. Evidence: proof:domain-identity-matrix and proof:tenant-storage.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation proves slug and custom-domain resolution, X-Forwarded-Host precedence, null outcomes for apex/reserved/unknown/malformed hosts, tenant prefix isolation, foreign-key rejection, and cross-tenant RLS counts of zero. Evidence: domain/storage/observability proofs.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Untrusted hosts resolve to null tenant context, absent/unverified custom domains are rejected, foreign tenant data is hidden or rejected, and provider failures degrade readiness rather than falling back to an unsafe tenant. Evidence: tenant resolver and storage proofs.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Isolation checks are guard behavior and do not emit mutation audit directly; audited mutations in tenant-scoped capabilities include tenant id/organisation id metadata and fail before cross-tenant writes. Evidence: audit-bearing capability proofs.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Isolation readiness is proven by live substrate checks for resolver, RLS, storage prefixes, and tenant-scoped provider probes; an unavailable provider is degraded/unreachable rather than treated as isolated. Evidence: proof inventory.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs include proof:domain-identity-matrix and proof:tenant-storage, with additional live RLS isolation in metering, search, observability, webhooks, events, and secret-store proofs. Proof level: 4 live substrate isolation validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must never infer tenant from client-side state alone, must display no-tenant/forbidden states for unresolved hosts, must scope every tenant table/action/filter to server-provided tenant context, and must not expose cross-tenant search/storage/log/provider data.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0029; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

semanticCompleteness.permissions: Permissions are enforced after tenant resolution; unresolved/apex/reserved hosts do not grant tenant context, tenant actors only access their organisation rows/prefixes, and operator bypass requires explicit rls_bypass/system role. Evidence: domain-identity, tenant-storage, and RLS proofs.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are host-identity resolution, tenant-aware route context, storage prefixes, RLS-backed repository ports, and readiness proofs for tenant-scoped substrates. Evidence: proof:domain-identity-matrix and proof:tenant-storage.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation proves slug and custom-domain resolution, X-Forwarded-Host precedence, null outcomes for apex/reserved/unknown/malformed hosts, tenant prefix isolation, foreign-key rejection, and cross-tenant RLS counts of zero. Evidence: domain/storage/observability proofs.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Untrusted hosts resolve to null tenant context, absent/unverified custom domains are rejected, foreign tenant data is hidden or rejected, and provider failures degrade readiness rather than falling back to an unsafe tenant. Evidence: tenant resolver and storage proofs.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Isolation checks are guard behavior and do not emit mutation audit directly; audited mutations in tenant-scoped capabilities include tenant id/organisation id metadata and fail before cross-tenant writes. Evidence: audit-bearing capability proofs.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Isolation readiness is proven by live substrate checks for resolver, RLS, storage prefixes, and tenant-scoped provider probes; an unavailable provider is degraded/unreachable rather than treated as isolated. Evidence: proof inventory.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs include proof:domain-identity-matrix and proof:tenant-storage, with additional live RLS isolation in metering, search, observability, webhooks, events, and secret-store proofs. Proof level: 4 live substrate isolation validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must never infer tenant from client-side state alone, must display no-tenant/forbidden states for unresolved hosts, must scope every tenant table/action/filter to server-provided tenant context, and must not expose cross-tenant search/storage/log/provider data.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0029; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant lifecycle: provision, suspend, delete, export
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.capability: Tenant lifecycle: provision, suspend, delete, export
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: tenant lifecycle coordinates portable export, data, storage, realm, and DSR transitions

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Lifecycle operations are explicit: suspend coordinates data -> storage -> realm -> DSR after audit; delete exports first, then coordinates storage -> realm -> DSR -> data. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Tenant lifecycle state is represented by coordinated subsystem outcomes and export metadata: suspend returns coordinated [data, storage, realm, dsr]; delete returns coordinated [export, storage, realm, dsr, data] plus export digest/keyRef. Evidence: tenant lifecycle unit tests and runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Capability is operator-controlled by platform.tenants.* with system-admin actor context in proof and tests. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts actor fixture.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract source is TenantLifecycleCoordinator with exportTenant, suspendData, suspendStorage, suspendRealm, suspendDsr, deleteData, deleteStorage, deleteRealm, deleteDsr plus admin tenant route family. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Delete refuses to continue after a subsystem coordination failure; failed deleteRealm stops before DSR/data deletion. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Subsystem failures propagate as typed/ordinary errors and stop later destructive steps; deleteRealm failure leaves calls at export, delete:storage, delete:realm. Evidence: tenant lifecycle unit test failure case.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Suspend and delete emit audit before subsystem coordination; unit and runtime proof assert audit invocation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts; apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-lifecycle-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Runtime readiness is npm run proof:tenant-lifecycle, which proves audit and subsystem coordination order for suspend and delete. Evidence: package.json script proof:tenant-lifecycle.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts. Runtime proof: apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-lifecycle-runtime-proof.ts. Proof level: 3 state-machine/coordination validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: No new UI is required; V2 UI must expose suspend/delete/export lifecycle actions, audit-before-change expectations, export-first delete warning, and failure-stop semantics from this record and the TenantLifecycleCoordinator contract.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0066, ADR-0063; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Subsystem failures propagate as typed/ordinary errors and stop later destructive steps; deleteRealm failure leaves calls at export, delete:storage, delete:realm. Evidence: tenant lifecycle unit test failure case.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Suspend and delete emit audit before subsystem coordination; unit and runtime proof assert audit invocation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts; apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-lifecycle-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Runtime readiness is npm run proof:tenant-lifecycle, which proves audit and subsystem coordination order for suspend and delete. Evidence: package.json script proof:tenant-lifecycle.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-lifecycle.test.ts. Runtime proof: apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-lifecycle-runtime-proof.ts. Proof level: 3 state-machine/coordination validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: No new UI is required; V2 UI must expose suspend/delete/export lifecycle actions, audit-before-change expectations, export-first delete warning, and failure-stop semantics from this record and the TenantLifecycleCoordinator contract.

evidence.auditAction: n/a (reading history is not itself audited; the audit_events rows already record the audited actions)
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: History lifecycle is read existing audit, notification, meter, and incident sources, union them into a tenant-scoped projection, paginate results, redact metadata/payload/secret fields, and leave source rows unchanged. Evidence: history runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes history entries with id, source, type, title, occurredAt, actor/status summary where safe, total, limit, and offset; it deliberately excludes raw metadata, payloads, tokens, and secrets. Evidence: PostgresHistoryRepository proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant reads require tenant.audit.read for own history; operator selected-tenant reads require platform.audit.read_all. Evidence: capability record and route contract.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are GET /api/org/history and GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/history with limit/offset pagination and safe summary entries. Evidence: history proof and route metadata.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation proves multiple source types are included, tenant A/B isolation, no secret or metadata content in entries, no secret-bearing field names, limit/offset pagination, and source row count unchanged after query. Evidence: history runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Unavailable Postgres skips honestly in runtime proof; route/usecase failures surface as read errors without mutating sources or leaking raw metadata. Evidence: history proof behavior.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Reading history is not itself audited; history displays already-persisted audit/source rows and never writes source state. Evidence: proof validates no source mutation.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is always-on when Postgres source tables are available; live proof validates projection availability and honest skip when the backend is absent. Evidence: proof:history.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:history validates live Postgres union projection, redaction, tenant isolation, pagination, and read-only behavior. Proof level: 4 live substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose tenant/operator history feeds, source/type filters, pagination controls, safe summaries, no raw metadata/payload/secret display, permission-denied states, and source drilldowns only through owning capability views.

gaps.subject: Tenant host identity resolution
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing retention runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Host identity lifecycle is classify -> resolve: apex/reserved/invalid/malformed hosts stay global/no-tenant, tenant slug hosts resolve by organisation slug, active custom domains resolve by tenant_domains registry, and inactive/pending/disabled custom domains remain unresolved. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/domain-identity-matrix-runtime-proof.ts and tenant-custom-domain-resolution-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Host identity state uses kind apex|tenant_slug|reserved_subdomain|invalid_subdomain|possible_custom_domain|malformed plus resolved tenant context containing organisationId, slug, realmName, and hostSource. Evidence: @platform/domain-identity and apps/platform-api/src/server/tenant-resolver.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Host identity is read-only request context; protected route permissions are enforced later by the pipeline, while global routes reject apex subdomains. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/pipeline.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract is GET /api/host-identity and implicit Host/X-Forwarded-Host request context consumed by the BFF pipeline. Evidence: route table and domain-identity-matrix proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Classification covers port stripping, reserved slugs, malformed hosts, unknown apex subdomains, possible custom-domain hosts, and X-Forwarded-Host precedence. Evidence: packages/domain-identity tests and domain-identity-matrix runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Untrusted or unresolved hosts return null tenant context; global routes reject apex subdomains with apex_subdomain detail instead of silently serving global content. Evidence: tenant-resolver and pipeline tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: No mutation audit applies because host identity resolution is a read-only derivation; failed resolution is logged by tenant-resolver/pipeline warnings. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/tenant-resolver.ts.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready when resolver code and registry are available; runtime proof requires local Postgres for live resolution and skips only when no seeded organisation exists. Evidence: domain-identity-matrix runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:domain-identity-matrix and proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution. Proof level: 4 live local Postgres resolver validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must consume host identity as context only: show resolved tenant/global state, prevent tenant UI on unresolved hosts, and present custom-domain source distinctly from slug source without granting permissions.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0231; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant domain activation (auth-client)
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Activation lifecycle is inactive -> active only after verified ownership, audit emit, and AuthClientDomainPort.addRedirectOrigin success; deactivation active -> inactive removes the origin and clears canonical/routing state. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts and tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is tenant_domains.authClientStatus inactive|active plus authClientActivatedAt, canonical, routingStatus, and disabledAt guards. Evidence: TenantDomainRecord and tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Activation/deactivation require tenant.domains.write; the domain-only operation no longer requires tenant.auth.settings.write. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase comments and capability record.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/org/domains/:id/activate and corresponding deactivation route, backed by activateDomainAuthClient/deactivateDomainAuthClient. Evidence: routes and usecase contract.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Activation refuses not_found, not_verified, and already_active before audit or external mutation; deactivation refuses not_found and not_active. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed result variants are ok, not_found, not_verified, already_active, and not_active; thrown AuthClientDomainPort errors leave registry inactive because markAuthClientActive is not called. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Activation and deactivation emit tenant_domains.auth_client.activated/deactivated before the Keycloak port mutation, with safe domain-only metadata. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Invariant-ready after verified ownership and successful auth-client mutation; activation does not imply routing or TLS readiness. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/tenant-domain-lifecycle.test.ts. Runtime proof: proof:tenant-domain-canonical. Proof level: 4 live registry validation with injected auth-client port.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose activate/deactivate actions only when permitted, show inactive/active states, block activation until ownership is verified, clear canonical/routing presentation after deactivation, and show typed guard failures.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0232; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant canonical domain set/unset
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is
evidence.acceptablePartialRouteReason: Canonical cutover is proven in the self-contained local stack via redirect policy transition and redirectActive exposure; public DNS remains separately limited by V1C-09.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Canonical lifecycle is guarded set/unset: not_verified -> refused, auth_client_inactive -> refused, routing_not_proven -> refused, set flips canonical true and redirect_slug_to_canonical, second set atomically replaces the first, unset clears the flag, and deactivation clears canonical. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is canonical boolean plus canonicalAt and redirectPolicy, constrained to at most one enabled canonical row per tenant; canonical does not upgrade routingStatus or tlsStatus. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof and tenant_domains registry.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Canonical set/unset require tenant.domains.write. Evidence: capability record and tenant-domain lifecycle usecases.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/org/domains/canonical, backed by setCanonicalDomain and unsetCanonicalDomain. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Set canonical requires verified ownership, active auth client, and proven local routing; unset requires existing domain; second canonical replacement preserves the one-canonical invariant. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed result variants are ok, not_found, not_verified, auth_client_inactive, and routing_not_proven. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set/unset emit tenant_domains.canonical.set/unset audit events with domain and redirectPolicy metadata before registry mutation. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase and unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is local canonical cutover only; public DNS/TLS remain explicitly deferred and canonical is a marker that does not imply public routing/TLS readiness. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:tenant-domain-canonical validates live registry guards, redirect policy, atomic replacement, unset, and deactivation clearing. Proof level: 4 live substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose one canonical marker per tenant, set/unset actions, guard-specific disabled/error states, redirect policy state, public DNS/TLS deferral labels, and canonical clearing after deactivation.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0232; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant canonical domain set/unset
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing tenant isolation runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is
evidence.acceptablePartialRouteReason: Canonical cutover is proven in the self-contained local stack via redirect policy transition and redirectActive exposure; public DNS remains separately limited by V1C-09.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Canonical lifecycle is guarded set/unset: not_verified -> refused, auth_client_inactive -> refused, routing_not_proven -> refused, set flips canonical true and redirect_slug_to_canonical, second set atomically replaces the first, unset clears the flag, and deactivation clears canonical. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State is canonical boolean plus canonicalAt and redirectPolicy, constrained to at most one enabled canonical row per tenant; canonical does not upgrade routingStatus or tlsStatus. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof and tenant_domains registry.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Canonical set/unset require tenant.domains.write. Evidence: capability record and tenant-domain lifecycle usecases.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/org/domains/canonical, backed by setCanonicalDomain and unsetCanonicalDomain. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Set canonical requires verified ownership, active auth client, and proven local routing; unset requires existing domain; second canonical replacement preserves the one-canonical invariant. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed result variants are ok, not_found, not_verified, auth_client_inactive, and routing_not_proven. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set/unset emit tenant_domains.canonical.set/unset audit events with domain and redirectPolicy metadata before registry mutation. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase and unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is local canonical cutover only; public DNS/TLS remain explicitly deferred and canonical is a marker that does not imply public routing/TLS readiness. Evidence: tenant-domain-canonical runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:tenant-domain-canonical validates live registry guards, redirect policy, atomic replacement, unset, and deactivation clearing. Proof level: 4 live substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose one canonical marker per tenant, set/unset actions, guard-specific disabled/error states, redirect policy state, public DNS/TLS deferral labels, and canonical clearing after deactivation.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0232; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant custom-domain auth callback
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.v2Target: carry-forward-blocked: derivation reused; live brokered login external
evidence.externalProofLimitation: Live brokered login on a custom domain requires a real external IdP and is not provable in the self-contained V2 stack. The derivation is delivered and proven (proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin); the live end-to-end proof is carried as the separate not-applicable-final capability [Real IdP login simulation] / [OIDC login simulation]. Not a V2-cut blocker.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Auth callback derivation lifecycle is request host -> tenant resolution -> login redirect derivation: active custom domains use tenant realm and custom callback origin; verified-but-inactive and unknown custom hosts fall back to platform realm; slug hosts keep tenant realm on slug origin. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State depends on tenant_domains ownershipStatus verified and authClientStatus active plus resolved realmName tenant-{orgId}; inactive/unknown custom host state is untrusted for callback derivation. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Domain callback origin eligibility is controlled by tenant.domains.write lifecycle operations; login derivation itself is request-context based and does not grant tenant permissions without authentication. Evidence: tenant-domain lifecycle and auth handler proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract is derived realm/redirect_uri for /auth/login and /auth/callback, with redirect_uri=https://{custom}/auth/callback only for active custom domains. Evidence: handleAuthLogin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Active custom domain must be verified and auth-client active; verified-but-inactive and unknown custom domains cannot claim callback origin; slug hosts remain valid. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Inactive or unknown custom hosts degrade to platform realm/callback instead of throwing or trusting the custom host; real IdP token exchange remains separately not-applicable in the self-contained stack. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin proof and externalProofLimitation.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: The derivation path is read-only; audit applies to the domain activation/deactivation mutations that make the callback origin eligible. Evidence: tenant-domain-lifecycle usecase audit events.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is deterministic derivation proof for local Postgres/Redis; live brokered login is external and explicitly carried as not-applicable-final rather than fake-passed. Evidence: tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin validates real handleAuthLogin against live local Postgres and Redis for active, inactive, unknown, and slug host cases. Proof level: 4 live handler/substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must show custom-domain auth callback eligibility only for active domains, display the exact callback origin, explain inactive/unknown fallback states, and keep real IdP login proof separate from derivation readiness.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0232; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin validates real handleAuthLogin against live local Postgres and Redis for active, inactive, unknown, and slug host cases. Proof level: 4 live handler/substrate validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must show custom-domain auth callback eligibility only for active domains, display the exact callback origin, explain inactive/unknown fallback states, and keep real IdP login proof separate from derivation readiness.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0232; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

gaps.subject: Tenant service clickthrough policy
gaps.message: data-owning capability missing retention runtime proof

evidence.contract: derived forward-auth sets + Caddyfile reconciliation; GET /admin/clickthrough
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Clickthrough lifecycle is classify each service, derive apex/tenant routes, decide access by actor role and requested tenant slug, reconcile Caddy forward-auth routes, render allowed URLs, and live-probe selected web-profile paths. Evidence: service-clickthrough tests and proofs.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes service id/resource/apexPath/tenantPath/classification, landingPath, devOnly flag, system-admin and tenant-admin resource sets, access decision granted/reason, and clickthrough URL. Evidence: service-clickthrough usecase/tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: System admins may access every exposed global service; tenant admins may access only tenant-scoped-safe services on their own slug; anonymous users access none; not-exposed services are denied to everyone. Evidence: service-clickthrough tests and runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are derived forward-auth resource sets, Caddy route reconciliation, clickthrough service list, and service-specific URLs with trailing slash/landingPath rules. Evidence: service-clickthrough tests and clickthrough proof.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation proves tenant-safe set exactly, cross-tenant denial, unknown-resource denial, not-exposed denial, Caddy route reconciliation, pgAdmin OIDC metadata config, LocalStack production URL lockout, ClickHouse landing path, and live web fallback behavior. Evidence: tests and proof scripts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Denied access returns explicit reasons such as not_exposed, unknown_resource, global_only, cross_tenant, or unauthenticated; unavailable web profile is skipped honestly in live route checks. Evidence: service-clickthrough usecase and proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Clickthrough policy is read/decision logic and emits no mutation audit; actual service operations are governed by the target service and auth proxy. Evidence: auditAction null.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant for pure policy and reconciler checks; live web-profile checks add route evidence when Caddy is reachable and otherwise report an honest skip. Evidence: proof:service-clickthrough-policy.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: service-clickthrough.test.ts. Runtime proofs: proof:service-clickthrough-policy and proof:clickthrough validate policy, route behavior, response redaction, and composed readiness inclusion. Proof level: 3 policy validation plus level 4 where live web routes are checked.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must show only allowed clickthrough links, preserve trailing slashes/landing paths, hide dev-only services in production, distinguish global-only and tenant-safe services, show denied reasons where appropriate, and never expose not-exposed services.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-ACT-0233; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004
